svg.title: How LLM cost is calculated — validated May 2026 prices
svg.text: you pay per token · input and output billed separately · prices verified May 2026
price per token = price per million ÷ 1,000,000
Level 2 — validated prices (May 2026 · standard interactive rate · not batch)
output tokens cost more — the model generated them, that takes more compute than reading your input
Level 3 — what does $1 actually buy? (gpt-4o-mini input · $0.15 per 1M · the cheapest in the table)
Level 4 — worked example from your actual demo (real token counts from the API response)
question: "What is a fixed deposit interest rate?"
Opus costs ~5× more than haiku for the identical question — same answer, very different bill.
Pick the cheapest model that gives you the quality you need. That decision is an architecture choice.
Level 5 — scale it up: 1 million users per day, same question
same question · same answer · opus costs 41× more than gpt-4o-mini at scale
model choice is an architecture decision — not just a quality decision
